limitations: Independent external-network denial was not tested.
Strict tracing of every ephemeral host write was not performed.
Window-ready startup latency was not instrumented.
Cross-host, cross-user, Windows-native, and long-term restoration remain untested.
The public fixture redacts the private account-bound save path and contains no payload.

tests.evidence: Archived wine and wineserver reported Wine 11.0 successfully without invoking Bottles.
The Full Archive and runner were verified during extraction; 2429 and 8821 members were materialized.
All 1759 archived runner symlinks resolved within the restored runner.
dosdevices/c: was regenerated as a relative link to ../drive_c and z: was omitted.
Two accepted persistent-state items were restored and verified.
Version 1.06, existing-save loading, video, audio, controller input, and gameplay were observed.
The generated launcher reused the materialization and completed a second functional session.
The second session recorded game process rc 0 and wineserver wait rc 0.
A changed save was detected; default removal was classified unsafe.
Current save and identity were exported, independently verified, and the registered materialization was removed.

tests.notes: The direct-Wine acceptance did not independently prove denial of external networking.
The accepted materialization was not tested under another host, user, or filesystem.

tests.evidence: Receipt play-61fce299f05dc289c4fdc2d79c2e2630 recorded a complete Direct-Wine session using WINEDLLOVERRIDES=mscoree=d;winemenubuilder.exe=d. No Wine Mono prompt appeared; the existing save loaded and launcher, game process, and Wineserver returned 0.
